llm.description:
Shared LLM configuration. Consumed by reranker-llm, ner-llm, summarization-llm,
VLM OCR, and any future LLM-backed capability. Off by default — leaving
`api_key` `Unset` short-circuits any LLM-backed feature.

max_file_bytes.description: Skip files larger than this. Prevents minified-bundle stalls.

skip_submodules.description:
When true (default), the scanner skips paths under any submodule root listed in
`.gitmodules`. Set to false to recurse into submodule working trees — useful only
if you want one combined index across the parent + its embedded repos.

eager_l2.description:
When true (default), the scanner runs L2 extraction (calls + docs) inline with L1.
L2 populates the `calls_by_callee` Fjall partition that drives `find_references` and
`find_callers`. Flipping to `false` halves the scan budget on large repos at the cost
of empty reference-search results until a foreground L2 pass is triggered (or the
existing on-demand `query::file_outline_l2` lazy path runs).

debounce_ms.description: Coalesce file events within this window (milliseconds).

file_map_lru.description: Maximum number of extracted FileMaps to keep hot in memory.

DocumentsConfig.description:
Top-level `[documents]` table. Each sub-config has `#[serde(default)]` so
adding a new tier never breaks older TOML files.

enabled.description: Master switch. Only meaningful when the `documents` cargo feature is compiled in.

mime_allowlist.description: MIME-type allowlist. Empty = accept anything kreuzberg can handle.

embedding_preset.description: Kreuzberg embedding preset name. Defaults to "balanced".

embed.description: Generate embeddings (`true`) or skip vector storage entirely (`false`).

language.description: Language detection + preferred languages for chunking / extraction.

ocr.description: OCR backend selection for image / scanned-PDF inputs.

output.description: Response wire format for the document MCP tools.

DocLanguageConfig.description:
Language detection + preferred-language list for the document tier. Named
`DocLanguageConfig` to avoid colliding with the per-tree-sitter-language
`LanguageConfig` (which is the scanner's per-grammar override map).

auto_detect.description:
Run kreuzberg's language detector. On by default; flip off when every doc
in the corpus is the same known language.

detected_languages.description:
User-specified preferred languages (ISO 639-3, e.g. `"fra"`, `"deu"`).
Drives chunking-tokenizer choice and biases the detector.

enabled.description:
Master switch — off by default; the reranker model download + per-query
latency means users should opt in explicitly.

preset.description:
Kreuzberg reranker preset name (`bge-reranker-base` is the small default;
`bge-reranker-large` and `bge-reranker-v2-m3` are heavier alternatives).

top_k.description:
How many hits to rerank. The vector search returns `top_k` candidates
which the cross-encoder then reorders.

enabled.description: Master switch — off by default; YAKE / RAKE add ingest-time CPU cost.

algorithm.description:
Algorithm: YAKE (statistical, multi-language) or RAKE (rapid automatic
keyword extraction).

count.description: Maximum keywords to extract per document.

yake_params.description: Optional YAKE tuning (passed through to kreuzberg unchanged).

rake_params.description: Optional RAKE tuning (passed through to kreuzberg unchanged).

enabled.description:
Master switch — off by default; ONNX backend downloads gline-rs weights on
first use, LLM backend costs API tokens.

backend.description:
Backend selection. `onnx` uses gline-rs locally; `llm` routes through the
shared `[llm]` config.

model.description: Override the ONNX model name (kreuzberg has a default catalogue when unset).

entity_types.description:
Whitelist of entity types to surface (`PERSON`, `LOC`, `ORG`, …). Empty
means "use the backend default".

strategy.description: Strategy: extractive (TextRank, no LLM) or abstractive (routed via `[llm]`).

max_chars.description: Soft cap on summary length in characters.

backend.description:
Backend selection. `tesseract` is the default; `paddle` for CJK-heavy
corpora; `vlm` routes through `[llm]` for vision-language OCR.

languages.description: Tesseract / PaddleOCR language packs (ISO 639-3 codes like `"eng"`).

format.description: Wire format used by document-tier MCP responses.

enabled.description: Master switch. Only meaningful when the `memory` cargo feature is compiled in.

scope_strategy.description: How to derive the scope key for an opened repository.

oneOf.description:
Use normalized `origin` remote URL when available, else fall back to the
workdir realpath. This is the recommended default — clones of the same
repo share memory across machines.
Always use the workdir realpath. Separates clones of the same repo.

respect_robots_txt.description:
Honour `robots.txt` when fetching pages. Default `true`. Override only
for hosts you control — flipping this off violates `robots.txt`
directives for every domain the crawler touches.

max_pages.description: Hard cap on pages visited in a single `web_crawl` call.

max_depth.description: Maximum link-following depth from the seed URL during `web_crawl`.

max_body_size.description: Truncate response bodies above this many bytes before parsing.

user_agent.description:
User-Agent header sent with every request. Override to identify your
crawler to operators; the default includes the basemind release
version + the upstream repo URL so site operators can trace traffic.

LlmConfig.description:
Shared LLM credentials + model selection. Consumed by every LLM-backed
capability (ner-llm, summarization-llm, reranker-llm, VLM OCR).

provider.description:
Provider name (`openai`, `anthropic`, `vllm`, …). Maps to a kreuzberg
liter-llm client at the boundary.

api_key.description:
API key — either a literal (discouraged; keeps secrets in version control)
or an `{ env = "OPENAI_API_KEY" }` reference. `Unset` short-circuits any
LLM-backed feature.

base_url.description: Override the provider base URL (for self-hosted vLLM, Azure, …).

ApiKey.description:
Tri-state API key wrapper. Serde-deserialised as `null` (`Unset`), a plain
string (`Literal`), or a `{ env = "NAME" }` table (`Env`).

anyOf.description:
Literal value baked into config. Strongly discouraged for production —
`Env` keeps secrets out of source control.
`{ env = "OPENAI_API_KEY" }` — resolved at load time via `std::env::var`.
Missing / `null` — every LLM-backed feature treats this as "disabled".
